sanskrit: प्रकृतेः क्रियमाणानि गुणैः कर्माणि सर्वशः। अहङ्कारविमूढात्मा कर्ताहमिति मन्यते॥
transliteration: prakṛteḥ kriyamāṇāni guṇaiḥ karmāṇi sarvaśaḥ / ahaṅkāra-vimūḍhātmā kartāham iti manyate
literal_meaning: All actions are performed by the gunas of prakṛti; the self, deluded by ego, thinks 'I am the doer.'
contextual_meaning: The ego mis-attributes agency: the mind–body system (prakṛti) executes behavior, yet the self appropriates authorship, sustaining the illusion of personal control.
psychological_state: Illusory control maintained by ego-defensive attribution; underlying existential dread of not-being-in-control is warded off by over-identification with doer-ship.
trigger_scenario: A project manager micromanages every detail, takes sole credit for success, and feels crushing guilt when KPIs slip—unable to see the systemic, team, and market forces actually driving outcomes.
anchor_text: Let the body-mind act; you remain the calm, choiceless witness.
